resources.string: On first install, you must read and accept the agreement below before using the app.

Welcome to this software. Please read and understand the following before use. By tapping “Accept and Continue”, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and accepted all terms of this agreement.

1. Nature of the software
This software is open source and is primarily intended for learning, research, technical communication, and non-commercial testing related to NFC, RFID, tag data processing, and similar topics.
Parts of the source code, configuration, and related materials may be obtained from or synchronized with public repositories. The developer may deliver configuration files, notices, advertisement content, or other public runtime configuration from remote repositories or servers.

2. Usage statistics
To understand installation volume, app launches, version distribution, and compatibility, this software includes a basic usage counting feature. After installation and on subsequent launches, the app may send a locally generated unique install identifier together with necessary basic version and device information to a server for statistics, compatibility analysis, and maintenance purposes.
This feature is not intended to directly identify a natural person. However, absolute anonymity is not guaranteed. If you do not agree to such statistics, do not use this software.

3. Remote configuration
This software may fetch public configuration files from remote repositories or servers to update color mappings, type mappings, notices, advertisement content, jump links, and other runtime configuration. The developer does not guarantee continued availability, accuracy, security, timeliness, or permanent compatibility of remote configuration. Any issue caused by remote configuration changes, network failures, cache delays, or third-party service problems shall be evaluated and borne by the user.

4. Risks and disclaimer
This software is provided “as is”, without any express or implied warranty, including but not limited to availability, stability, merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, data integrity, or security.
Use of this software may involve NFC reading, writing, tag formatting, file import/export, data overwrite, and network access, and may lead to risks including but not limited to tag damage, write failure, data loss, inventory errors, device compatibility issues, configuration errors, third-party link risks, file overwrite, user mistakes, and other unforeseeable consequences.
The developer, contributors, and distributors shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, punitive, or consequential loss arising from use, misuse, reliance on, or inability to use this software.

5. Restrictions
Use this software only on devices, tags, data, and environments that you are authorized to operate. Do not use this software for illegal activity, infringement, destructive behavior, bulk attacks, unauthorized tag writing, bypassing access control, or any other improper purpose. Any legal liability, financial loss, or third-party dispute arising from user behavior shall be borne solely by the user.

6. Open-source and communication notice
You are welcome to study, research, improve, and discuss this software under the applicable open-source license, but you are responsible for reviewing the code and build artifacts yourself, and for all risks resulting from compiling, modifying, distributing, or using it. Third-party dependencies, repository content, external links, and remote configuration are not guaranteed to be maintained or warranted indefinitely by the developer.

7. Agreement updates
The developer may update this agreement when features, statistics, remote configuration mechanisms, or compliance requirements change. The updated agreement may be shown again on the first launch of a new version. If you do not agree to the updated terms, stop using the software.

If you do not agree to any of the above, tap “Decline and Exit”. Continuing to use the software means that you fully understand and accept the open-source nature of the software, the usage statistics feature, the remote configuration mechanism, and all related risks and disclaimers.
Tray UID missing, cannot save remaining.
Material weight missing, cannot set grams.
1. Keep the tag firmly against the phone NFC area. Do not move it while writing.
2. If writing or verification fails, remove the tag and tap again — the app will retry automatically.
3. Do not write to an existing tag. Duplicate tags may be recognized as the same spool.
4. Writing may fail. The author is not responsible for any consequences.
You need to recharge 999999999999 yuan to unlock 🤣🤣🤣
C Card modify ready: keep the target C card steady on the NFC area until complete.
Modifying C card, keep the tag steady, do not move...
This page is unrelated to filament data. It writes custom data to tags for extra use cases. It can also write other data to official Bambu tags, but you usually need to run Format Tag first.
Example: Hello NFC / Device ID / Notes
Hint: fill SSID, password can be empty for open networks, and security can be WPA/WEP/NONE. WiFi will be written as an NDEF text record in WIFI:... format.
Finish the current tap task first
NDEF write ready: keep the target tag steady on the NFC area until writing and verification finish.
Read all sector data and save files
Reading all data will slow scanning down. Files will be saved under Android/data/com.m0h31h31.bamburfidreader/rfid_file/self_xxxxx. Enable it?
Importing the database will overwrite the current local filament database. Continue?
This will delete all tag files under the current self directory and cannot be undone. Continue?
This will remove all tag records from the database and cannot be undone. Continue?
Auto-detect brand on tap and navigate to the matching reader (Bambu / Creality / Snapmaker)
Enable to show inventory and data pages
Automatically upload to shared server after reading full Bambu/Creality data
Hide tags that have been successfully copied
There is an announcement available. It is recommended to check it in the Settings page.
You found the version easter egg
More taps still will not add merit
The version label has been lit up
Select a tag package to import (.zip)
Format ready: keep the target tag steady on the NFC area until completion.
Finish or cancel the current write/verify task first
CUID test ready: keep the target tag steady on the NFC area until complete.
Writing in progress. Keep the tag steady on the NFC area.
Verifying in progress. Keep the tag steady on the NFC area.
Writing NDEF data. Keep the tag steady on the NFC area.
Writing complete. Remove the tag, then tap again to verify.
Left the copy page. The write task was stopped.
Finish the current tap task first
Tray ID %1$s already exists in inventory. Continue copying anyway?
Write ready: keep the target blank tag steady on the NFC area until completion.
Canceled: duplicate tray ID detected, writing not started.
Trying repair: matching shared file by UID...
Matched file found. Recovery write ready: %1$s
No data file found for UID=%1$s. Please choose one manually on the Copy page.
Shared data is already refreshing. Please wait.
Outbound failed: tray ID not read.
This device does not support NFC.
NFC is off. Enable it in system settings.
Enable Bambu filament tag copy page
Enable Creality filament RFID read/write page
Tap a tag to read automatically
Fill in filament info, then tap a tag to write
Ready to write — tap a tag
Tap anywhere on the photo to sample that color
This feature is not 100% accurate. Please rely on the actual AMS response as the definitive result.
This page is designed exclusively for Creality 3D filament tags. Only tag reading and writing are supported — fully independent from inventory, data, and other features.

This filament data fails AMS recognition, or if the AMS reports it cannot identify this tag, please submit a report. Reported tags will be marked as anomalous in the shared library. Please report honestly — do not submit false reports.
This tag may be banned by AMS (%d reports). Copy with caution.
Failed to save nickname, please try again later
Please allow installation from unknown sources in system settings first
Update check failed, please try again later
After successfully sharing tags, you can enjoy shared data from others. Without sharing, you cannot access others\' shared data. Are you sure you want to disable it?
Open SnapMaker U1 Filament Tag Clone Page
Select Tag and Write to Card
